summary: Validated /home.html mobile responsiveness across 5 viewports (360, 390, 412, 600, 768) after Fix-2 (clamp() + inline font-size override attribute selectors + cache-buster v=1782334245). ALL VIEWPORTS PASS.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix-2 strategy is solid: @media (max-width:1024px) with clamp() + attribute selectors [style*="font-size:40"] correctly override inline styles that the previous class-only media query missed.
Cache-buster query string (?v=1782334245) on CSS link is being served correctly in home.html.
Layout is clean on all tested widths: PROVA NACIONAL DOCENTE logo fits ~60% viewport, paragraph text wraps, no horizontal scroll. Nav links Cronograma/Orientações/Dúvidas? are reasonably sized.

If user STILL sees a desorganized layout in their real Android device, the most likely remaining cause is browser HTTP cache holding the OLD CSS without ?v=1782334245. Recommend they pull-to-refresh or close/reopen the tab; CDN/edge cache may also need invalidation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 22: /home.html responsive fixes fully validated. body.scrollWidth==window.innerWidth on 360/390/412/600/768. No element has computed font-size >32px. No element overflows right edge. CSS file 200 OK and contains @media (max-width: 1024px) plus inline attribute-selector overrides. Cache-buster v=1782334245 is being served. If user reports persisting issue it is client-side cache, not code.
rca_of_the_issue: Previous fix (iter 20/21) only targeted CSS classes inside @media (max-width:768px). Inline style='font-size:40px/32px/30px' on home.html elements bypassed the rules, and viewports 769-1024 (foldables/small tablets) also weren't matched. Fix-2 adds @media (max-width:1024px) using clamp() + attribute selectors that pick up inline styles, and adds cache-buster on CSS href. Validated: 0 overflow, 0 unstyled inline overrides across 5 viewports.
